title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Contract Workshop
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds testable command lifecycle and function map from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of testable command lifecycle and function map while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Main Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1989 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Main Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Main Boundary, keep Main Boundary connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-command-trace-lbqyjt
# Competency: bookbot-command-trace.
# Case 9203: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_command_trace_lbqyjt(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_lbqyjt = "lbqyjt-tjyqbl-9203"
    assert evidence_variant_lbqyjt
    evidence_axes_lbqyjt = (
        "corpuslbqyjt",
        "policytjyqbl",
        "revision9203",
        "commandlbqyjt9203",
        "reporttjyqbl9203",
        "test9203lbqyjt",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_lbqyjt)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"
